title: Fresh Realistic Harness Hardening
subtitle: Prove the standalone harness on a fresh realistic living doc, with fewer supervising-session fixes.

epicIssue.title: Epic: fresh realistic harness hardening run

objective: Prove that the standalone living-doc harness can take a fresh realistic living doc from start to objective closure with minimal supervising-session intervention, while preserving contract evidence, choosing inference units correctly, and exposing any remaining blocker as a ticket with traceable run evidence.
successCondition: A fresh realistic living doc can be started through the standalone lifecycle controller, progress through worker, reviewer, proof/repair, commit or PR-review gates when required, closure review, and post-flight narrative without relying on the supervising chat session for state or decisions. The run either closes because the objective is proven by contract-bound inference units and controller artifacts, or stops with a blocker issue that cites the exact run, contract, raw-log, and artifact evidence needed to continue.

in: One fresh realistic living-doc run used as the hardening proof surface.
Controller routing after ambiguous reviewer verdicts.
Commit intent and PR-review evidence continuity.
Repair, proof-route, worker-continuation, blocker, and closure-review selection.
Dashboard visibility into routing decisions, contract arrows, unit instances, logs, and living-doc change evidence.
Post-flight decision narrative after terminal closed or blocked state.
out: A general policy registry for all future lifecycle policies.
Remote production deployment of the dashboard.
Solving every future harness failure class before it appears in a real run.
Treating a manually patched previous run as the fresh-run proof.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Run docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard-terminal-state-reconciliation.json through the standalone lifecycle controller with the local harness tool profile and required-before-closure PR-review policy, then let emitted contracts decide worker, reviewer, repair, proof, commit, PR-review, closure, blocker, and post-flight units.

supervisorMay: watch dashboard and raw artifact logs
create or approve tickets when blockers are found
commit source/doc changes after the harness produces objective-scoped evidence
supervisorMustNot: use the supervising chat session as the lifecycle decision maker
manually route around controller contracts without recording a process defect
claim closure from a useful slice while fresh-run proof is missing
reuse the prior PR-policy run as the fresh realistic proof

data.name: Fresh-run hardening prepared
data.summary: The objective, acceptance criteria, epic, subtickets, issue orbit, controller/contract expectations, and fresh realistic test living doc are defined. The next move is a standalone harness run against docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard-terminal-state-reconciliation.json.
data.nextStep: Start the harness on docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard-terminal-state-reconciliation.json with required-before-closure PR review.

data.name: Use one living doc and one epic
data.decision: The hardening work is held by one living doc and one GitHub epic, with subtickets for failure classes exposed or targeted by the fresh run.
data.reason: The objective must stay accountable as a lifecycle outcome, while real failures need separate traceable tickets.

data.name: Fresh run is required
data.decision: The prior PR-policy lifecycle closure is useful evidence of capability, but it is not the proof for this objective.
data.reason: That lifecycle closed after many harness fixes. This objective tests whether the hardened system now works on a fresh realistic doc with less supervising-session intervention.

data.name: Real blockers become tickets
data.decision: When the harness cannot continue, the correct terminal behavior is a blocker issue with traceable run evidence, not a silent loop or manual workaround.
data.reason: The system has to expose its unsolved boundary so the next run can resume from durable evidence.

data.name: Fresh realistic test doc exists
data.criterion: A new or deliberately selected small realistic living doc exists with a concrete source-system objective, tight acceptance criteria, proof routes, and scope boundaries; it starts open and cannot close by merely rendering HTML or updating its own text.
data.proofRequired: Source JSON, rendered HTML, issue reference, and objective-readiness check showing the doc is executable and non-trivial.
data.proofObserved: Created docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard-terminal-state-reconciliation.json and rendered docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard-terminal-state-reconciliation.html. The doc targets dashboard terminal-state reconciliation, starts open, names source surfaces and proof routes, and cannot close from rendering alone.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: create and run a fresh realistic living-doc test case

data.name: Run uses the standalone controller
data.criterion: The proof run is started through the standalone lifecycle controller and records lifecycle artifacts, unit contracts, raw JSONL log references, proof routes, and dashboard events without using the supervising chat session as state or decision source.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle result directory, events.jsonl, unit result artifacts, raw-log path references, dashboard graph, and no manual controller substitution in the run notes.

data.name: Reviewer verdicts route to the correct next unit
data.criterion: When reviewer inference says the objective is not closed, the controller selects worker, repair, proof route, commit intent, PR review, blocker, or closure review from explicit contract evidence and records the selection reason.
data.proofRequired: Post-review selection artifact, reviewer verdict, selected-unit input contract, and dashboard edge label for each crucial route.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: route ambiguous reviewer verdicts to the right inference unit
Hardening: select repair, proof route, or worker continuation from evidence

data.name: Evidence survives iteration boundaries
data.criterion: Evidence produced in earlier iterations remains available and validated in later iterations, including commit evidence, PR-review evidence, repair outputs, proof-route artifacts, raw-log inspection references, and closure-review inputs.
data.proofRequired: Later-iteration hard facts and contracts cite carried-forward artifacts with valid relative or absolute paths, validation status, and source iteration/run identity.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: conserve evidence across lifecycle iterations

data.name: Commit and PR-review gates stay connected
data.criterion: When source or doc changes require commit intent or PR review, the lifecycle records which paths changed, which commit or diff was reviewed, which policy required review, and whether the closure gate is satisfied or blocked.
data.proofRequired: Commit-intent result, PR-review input/output contract, side-effect evidence, policy state, and closure-review basis all refer to matching changed evidence.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: make commit and PR-review continuity explicit

data.name: Dashboard explains routing and evidence
data.criterion: The dashboard represents each inference unit as a run instance, keeps contract labels attached to arrows, shows living-doc change arrows separately, and lets an operator inspect the contract, evidence paths, raw-log reference, and selected log without losing scroll position.
data.proofRequired: Dashboard screenshot or E2E proof from the fresh run showing graph, side pane, edge details, living-doc node, and readable log pane.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: improve dashboard observability for routing decisions

data.name: Terminal state is closed or blocked with evidence
data.objectiveTerm: objective closure; exposing any remaining blocker
data.criterion: The lifecycle stops only when the objective is proven closed by contract-bound inference units and controller artifacts, or when it creates a blocker issue that cites the exact run, unit, contract, raw log, and artifact evidence needed to continue.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle-result.json with closed or terminal-blocked classification, closure-review or blocker contract, and linked GitHub issue if blocked.

data.name: Post-flight narrative explains the run
data.criterion: After terminal closed or blocked state, a post-flight inference unit generates a static HTML narrative explaining the crucial routing decisions and stop reason from persisted artifacts and raw-log paths.
data.proofRequired: Post-flight unit input contract, output HTML, and cited lifecycle/reviewer/unit/proof artifacts.

ticketIds.title: Hardening: generate post-run decision narrative as a post-flight inference unit

data.name: Epic: fresh realistic harness hardening run
Make commit and PR-review continuity explicit
Select repair, proof route, or worker continuation from evidence
Create and run a fresh realistic living-doc test case
Improve dashboard observability for routing decisions
Generate post-run decision narrative as a post-flight inference unit
Fresh hardening loop
data.claim: A fresh realistic living doc should move through the standalone harness until the objective closes or a blocker issue is created from traceable evidence.
data.semanticRole: Control model for the hardening run.
data.validationQuestion: Can the lifecycle explain every next-unit decision from contracts rather than supervising-session judgment?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the run closes from a manual patch, repeats a stale unit without new evidence, or stops without blocker evidence.

diagrams.title: Fresh Realistic Harness Loop
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Doc["Fresh realistic living doc"] --> Controller["Standalone lifecycle controller"]
  Controller --> Worker["worker inference unit"]
  Worker --> Reviewer["reviewer inference unit"]
  Reviewer -->|not closed with reason| Selection["controller next-unit selection"]
  Selection --> Commit["commit-intent"]
  Selection --> PR["pr-review"]
  Selection --> Repair["repair skill unit"]
  Selection --> Proof["proof route"]
  Selection --> Continue["worker continuation"]
  Commit --> Reviewer
  PR --> Reviewer
  Repair --> Reviewer
  Proof --> Reviewer
  Continue --> Reviewer
  Reviewer -->|closure candidate| Closure["closure-review"]
  Closure -->|approved| Closed["closed"]
  Closure -->|blocked| Issue["blocker issue with run evidence"]

data.name: Contract evidence chain
data.claim: Every inference unit receives an input contract and produces an output contract that either changes the living doc, changes the lifecycle state, or gives the controller evidence to select the next unit.
data.validationQuestion: Can later iterations validate evidence created by earlier iterations without rerunning or fabricating it?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if carried evidence lacks a source run, validation status, or matching gate.

diagrams.title: Contract Evidence Chain
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Input["input contract"] --> Unit["contract-bound inference unit"]
  Unit --> Output["output contract"]
  Output --> Artifacts["artifacts + raw JSONL paths"]
  Artifacts --> HardFacts["controller hard facts"]
  HardFacts --> NextInput["next input contract"]
  NextInput --> NextUnit["next inference unit"]
  Artifacts --> Dashboard["dashboard graph and side pane"]
  Artifacts --> Narrative["post-flight narrative"]

data.name: Objective readiness
data.claim: The hardening objective is concrete enough to drive implementation and test execution.
data.evidenceRequired: Living doc objective, acceptance criteria, issue orbit, rendered HTML, and explicit out-of-scope boundaries.
data.closureRelevance: Necessary starting proof; not sufficient for objective closure.

data.name: Fresh standalone run proof
data.claim: The harness can run a fresh realistic doc without borrowing decisions from the supervising chat session.
data.evidenceRequired: Lifecycle result, event log, raw JSONL references, unit contracts, proof-route artifacts, dashboard state, and terminal closed or blocked classification.

data.name: Post-flight decision narrative
data.claim: After the run stops, a separate inference unit can explain why each important unit ran and why the lifecycle stopped.
data.evidenceRequired: Static HTML narrative generated from run artifacts and raw-log paths.
data.closureRelevance: Explains the proof and exposes remaining risk.
